idiom: un día sí y otro también

1: Literally: occurring on one day and the next as well; happening on many consecutive days or on alternating days.
2: Frequently; repeatedly; over and over; all the time.

1: Literally, 'one or another time'; a single occasional occurrence.

1: Literally, 'one and another time'; indicating successive or repeated occurrences.

1: to be relevant or pertinent to the matter under discussion; to apply to the case at hand.
to be extremely convenient, timely, or suitable; to be exactly what is needed.
to occur or proceed under unfavorable circumstances; to be in a run of bad luck or to go badly.
to be perfectly suited or opportune for someone; to come at the right time or fit someone exactly.

idiom: vivir por encima de sus posibilidades

1: Literally, alive and wriggling or vigorous.

1: Used to indicate that a list or enumeration ends there, implying no more items or details will be added; equivalent to 'and that's it' or 'and that's all'.

Literally, given that we are (here or in this situation); used to point out the current circumstance.

1: Literally, 'what fault do I have?'; a rhetorical question used to deny personal blame or responsibility for a situation.
